claims.rationale: Doctors Trofimov and Kaznacheev at the Institute of Experimental Medicine (Novosibirsk) did run Kozyrev-mirror experiments in Siberia in 1990-1991 and publish results; the outer framing matches their own accounts, though the 'ESP enhancement' interpretation is their claim, not independently verified.

claims.text: In 1958 Russian physicist Nikolai Kozyrev reported volcanic activity on the Moon, a claim first dismissed but later confirmed by Apollo-era lunar sample returns.

evidence.quote:
at
first rejected but later proven true in
1958 he reported volcanic activity on
the moon at first his claim was
dismissed by the scientific Community

claims.rationale: Nikolai Kozyrev's 1958 spectroscopic observation of a gas emission from the crater Alphonsus is a documented event — his claim of transient lunar volcanism was controversial but later supported by TLP catalogs and Apollo-era follow-up.

claims.text: Kozyrev proposed that time itself is a physical energy that can be sped up, slowed down, or reversed, and that it propagates instantaneously through an invisible medium he called the ether.

evidence.quote:
ed time is a physical
Force actively participating in the
universe's existence time is the
heartbeat of the universe it creates
energy that impacts matter and space

claims.rationale: This is the core of Kozyrev's published 'causal mechanics' theory of time as energy — documented in his own papers. Whether the theory is physically correct is a separate question.

claims.text: On February 27, 1983, just as he was about to test a new time-mirror prototype, Nikolai Kozyrev suddenly and mysteriously died.

claims.rationale: Kozyrev died in 1983 — this is a documented biographical fact. The 'mysteriously' framing is the host's editorializing; the cause of death was kidney failure, as the host acknowledges with 'kidney' one cue later.

claims.text: Project Stargate was an American intelligence program that explored psychic abilities and specifically concentrated on remote viewing.

evidence.quote:

claims.rationale: Stargate was declassified in 1995; CIA documents (89,000+ pages released) confirm the remote-viewing focus. Run out of Fort Meade from 1972 to 1995 under successive names (SCANATE, Grill Flame, etc.).

claims.text: In Kozyrev-mirror telepathy trials between Dixon and Novosibirsk (1,400 miles apart), the image-transmission success rate rose from 0-10% in quiet geomagnetic conditions to 30-45% during solar storms.

claims.rationale: These claimed success rates come directly from Trofimov/Kaznacheev's book and their own unreplicated experiments. They have not been independently reproduced by mainstream parapsychology.

claims.text: In a follow-up experiment with two sending mirrors and 5,000 participants across 12 countries, the image-reception success rate during active magnetosphere conditions reportedly reached 95%.

evidence.quote:

claims.rationale: A 95% telepathic image-transmission rate across 12 countries is an extraordinary claim with no independent replication. It comes only from the Trofimov/Kaznacheev account and would overturn decades of parapsychology null results.

claims.text: Among the roughly 2,000 symbols Kozyrev-mirror participants reported seeing, a linguist found that 80% came from ancient cultures, most frequently ancient Sumerian.

evidence.quote:

ancient cultures in fact the most

months and exceede

claims.rationale: The '80% ancient-Sumerian' statistic is asserted only by the Kaznacheev book; it's unverified and suffers from obvious methodological problems (confirmation bias, post-hoc classification of symbols).

ca

claims.rationale: Self-reported phenomenology from suggestible participants in sensory-deprivation mirrors; results match standard hallucination literature for such conditions rather than confirming external entities.

claims.rationale: The CUNY Advanced Science Research Center group (Alù et al.) published a genuine 2023 Nature Physics paper on observing electromagnetic time reflections in a switched metamaterial — a real result, though it does not validate Kozyrev's metaphysics.

claims.rationale: The 61% vs 8% earthquake-prediction comparison comes from Trofimov's own papers; mainstream seismology does not accept his method and has not replicated the claimed accuracy.

claims.text: Mainstream science does not accept Kozyrev's ether-based theory of time or the parapsychology results claimed from Kozyrev-mirror experiments.

claims.rationale: Modern physics rejects a luminiferous ether (per Michelson-Morley and subsequent tests); modern parapsychology has not replicated the mirror results. The host acknowledges this in the verdict section.

dependencies.rationale: [alternative] mainstream physics provides alternative, ether-free explanations for the phenomena Kozyrev invoked time-energy to explain.
[undercuts] lack of independent replication of the extraordinary 95% figure undermines the result.
